# Kaola-Workflow Roadmap

This file mirrors active unfinished work. GitHub issues are the source of truth when available.

## Active Work

| Issue | Title | Status | Workflow Project | Next Step |
|-------|-------|--------|------------------|-----------|
| #729 | bug(replan): empty or uncovered failed-review findings can activate a review-only child epoch | PARTIALLY SHIPPED (8370e577) — case (a) empty-frontier refusal landed and is mutation-pinned; 3 of 9 acceptance criteria met | — | SHIPPED: reviewSourceCarriesFrontier + typed refusal replan_source_findings_missing, bound at every reachable frontier (source read, authority verification, planner packet, stored-transaction entry at both phases, candidate re-author); 8/8 mutants killed; recovery row added to the repair-routing card. TWO LOAD-BEARING SCOPING CALLS to preserve in any follow-up — the guard is scoped to authority_kind review_outcome because diagnosis_to_build legitimately carries findings [] (pinned by a killed mutant), and the predicate is non-empty FINDINGS not non-empty OPEN frontier, because a progress-only failure legitimately carries an all-resolved frontier whose record of what failed is intact. REMAINING (6 of 9 ACs): AC2 planner packets carrying immutable finding ids/anchors/statuses/ownership data (buildPlannerPacket emits none today — zero hits for route_candidates/ownership_candidates/owning_node); AC3 child carry-forward reporting uncovered uid/path/node; AC4 review-only child cannot activate for an unresolved path-based finding; AC5 correctly mapped writer-to-certifier still activates; AC6 N-1 of N coverage refuses; AC7 crash-prefix/idempotent retry at the new coverage seam. Case (b) SEMANTIC COVERAGE deliberately unimplemented with the soundness argument recorded inline: every mechanical proxy at this seam is unsound (an anchor records where a defect was OBSERVED and may legally carry no path; fix_role is advisory not contractual; "child has any writer" does not even catch the live repro). A sound check needs the child plan to DECLARE which inherited finding uids each writer owns — a plan-grammar addition in the validator, so AC2 is its natural prerequisite. Minor: repairCandidateReauthorPrefix performs durable writes before the guard on one resume path (epoch is NOT consumed and phase does not advance, so AC1 holds, but "zero mutation" is not literally true there). |
| #730 | bug(repair-dispatch): reopened fixer receives IDs but not a digest-bound canonical finding brief | ATTEMPTED AND REJECTED 2026-07-22 (adversarial review, blocks_commit) — rework required; the #727 commit it was stacked with shipped separately | — | REWORK. The rejected attempt built the brief but left the OWNERSHIP-ABSENT path telling the authorized fixer to fix NOTHING: when no open finding routes to an owner (anchor-less schema-2 evidence_observation findings, findings anchored at a file no node declares, or a pre-#701 journal), the ownership bridge stays inert and the reopen proceeds on the graph-maximal proof alone — so the reopened writer IS responsible for the whole open frontier, yet buildRepairBrief emits an empty assigned-uid set. The flagship test asserted the exact behavior the issue forbids: §1 requires that if ANY open finding is unowned, ambiguously owned, or owned by another writer, direct repair must REFUSE and route to replan rather than silently omit it. Also correct on the redo: an evidence claim was false (the walkthrough was cited as a second frontier but greps zero for repair_brief), and for schema-2 the "reviewer's verbatim statement" is vacuous — describe() prefers row.raw and schema2RouteCandidates synthesizes raw as 'uid=<uid>', while canonical findings carry no human-readable statement field at all (only failure_class / trigger digests / anchors / proof_digest), so that claim holds only for schema-1 flat rows. Keep the audited scope trim: an existing backstop (closure re-review carries prior_findings forward and refuses without a per-uid resolution_json) already re-fails the gate on an unaddressed finding, so do NOT rebuild it and do NOT add a close-time proof that merely re-verifies it. The un-backstopped core is that consumed_by is set when the selected writer owns AT LEAST ONE open finding, not all of them. |
| #745 | bug(plan-validator): interior code-reviewer/security-reviewer walls still false-block on disjoint downstream writes — the surface-freshness rescue covers only adversarial-verifier, and their reopen remedy still dead-ends | PARTIALLY SHIPPED — the false-block half landed (merge window ending 7d0df364); the reopen-remedy half is deliberately unimplemented | — | SHIPPED: interiorSurfaceFresh widened to code-reviewer/security-reviewer, with the producer slice RECOMPUTED from the frozen graph (not trusted from the journal) and a final certifier counting as a live whole-candidate wall only when its ledger row is actually complete. REMAINING: the reopen remedy. Auto-parking a "provably pristine" sink was REJECTED as unsafe, not deferred — the sink runs main-session-direct and its first irreversible step writes no in-band marker, so a pristine sink cannot be distinguished from one that already committed/pushed/merged. A safe remedy needs an explicit sink-progress marker. Cross-epoch freshness (seal attempt absent after journal rotation) also still falls back to whole-candidate. |
| #756 | bug(finalize/reopen): finalize done-gate surfacing a required file outside a completed writer's frozen write set wedges a PASSED-review serial run (reopen strands, amend orphans journal, replan needs a failed attempt) | ready — filed 2026-07-22 from vrpai-cli #1005, with all six recovery dead-ends reproduced empirically | — | BATCH WITH the #745 reopen-remedy half — both are the same mechanism (reopen-node reachability in adaptive-node.js) and designing them separately risks two conflicting partial remedies. A run whose review PASSED wedges at finalize when a project done-gate (not modeled as a DAG node) surfaces a required code-relevant file outside every frozen write set: barrier-check refuses write_set_overflow, verdict-check refuses verdict_not_pass (the code-relevant file stales the certifier candidate; docs correctly do not), reopen-node on the writer refuses would_strand_completed_dependent, on the gate refuses would_orphan_in_progress then would_strand, write-set amend + freeze --repair refuses review_journal_plan_hash_mismatch, and replan refuses replan_source_journal_missing because it requires a FAILED-review attempt a passed review never produces. Three candidate directions in the issue: (1) a sanctioned expand-and-re-review primitive that does not require a prior failed attempt, (2) reopen-node tail-first cascade instead of a hard would_strand refusal, (3) a planner companion-file completeness lint. Acceptance requires the recovery keep the new file REVIEWED, not merely attributed. |

## Rules

- A separate roadmap/research session owns discovering and adding future work to GitHub issues.
- `workflow-next` fetches GitHub issues, mirrors active implementation work here, and advances one item per cycle.
- After each workflow cycle, refresh this file from issue state.
- Move completed workflow project folders to `kaola-workflow/archive/`.
- Close linked GitHub issues only after acceptance criteria pass.
